description: Failure-mode taxonomy for Verity benchmark proof attempts. Classification has two levels: (1) the outcome status reported by the harness/verifier, and (2) when a submitted proof fails to type-check, a Lean failure sub-mode parsed from the verifier output. This file is the data-driven source of truth consumed by scripts/classify_failures.py; it can be extended without code changes and is independent of benchmark versioning (it never feeds result_key).

matching.method: Outcome status is mapped directly from the verifier/harness status. When the outcome is 'lean_check_failed', the verifier output text is scanned against lean_failure_modes in array order; the first mode with any matching (case-insensitive substring) signature wins. Ordering is deliberate: more specific / more proximate causes are listed before 'unsolved_goals', which is the softest terminal state and is therefore matched last among real Lean errors.

outcome_statuses.label: Passed
outcome_statuses.description: The submitted proof type-checks and discharges every target with no trusted-axiom violation.

outcome_statuses.label: Lean check failed
outcome_statuses.description: A candidate proof was submitted but Lean rejected it. The specific Lean failure sub-mode is parsed from the verifier output.

outcome_statuses.label: Theorem missing
outcome_statuses.description: The required theorem/declaration is absent from the submission. The model did not produce the target statement (often gave up, renamed it, or never reached it).

outcome_statuses.label: Timeout
outcome_statuses.description: The verifier build or elaboration exceeded its time budget before reaching a verdict.

outcome_statuses.label: Dependency checkout failed
outcome_statuses.description: Infrastructure failure: a build dependency (e.g. a pinned git revision) could not be fetched. Not attributable to the model.

outcome_statuses.label: Harness error / incomplete
outcome_statuses.description: The run did not complete cleanly (harness_status != completed). Excluded from model-skill conclusions; tracked separately as an infrastructure/transport problem.

outcome_statuses.label: No submission
outcome_statuses.description: No candidate proof file was produced for the task.

lean_failure_modes.label: Used sorry / admit
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: The proof type-checks only because it leaves a hole. The model failed to find a real proof and stubbed it out.

lean_failure_modes.label: Syntax / parse error
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: The submitted Lean source does not parse. Often malformed tactic syntax, unbalanced brackets, or invalid term structure.

lean_failure_modes.label: Unknown identifier / constant
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: The model referenced a name that does not exist (hallucinated lemma/definition, wrong namespace, or a tactic that is not in scope).

lean_failure_modes.label: Type / elaboration mismatch
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: A term is well-formed syntactically but ill-typed or ill-elaborated: wrong argument types, mis-applied function, a field/projection that does not fit, or an expected type that still contains free variables/metavariables.

lean_failure_modes.label: Maximum recursion depth
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: Elaboration blew the recursion/stack limit, usually from an unguarded unfolding, runaway simp set, or non-terminating rewrite.

lean_failure_modes.label: Elaboration heartbeat timeout
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: Elaboration exceeded the per-declaration heartbeat budget (e.g. timeout at `whnf`). The proof term is too expensive to elaborate -- typically heavy definitional unfolding, `decide`, or large `simp` sets. Distinct from the outcome-level 'timeout', which is the verifier's wall-clock budget; the verifier reports these as lean_check_failed.

lean_failure_modes.label: Decision procedure could not close goal
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: An automation tactic (omega/linarith/decide/ring/grind/...) was applied but could not discharge the goal — typically the goal is outside its fragment or needs manual setup first.

lean_failure_modes.label: simp made no progress
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: A simp call did not rewrite anything. The model expected a simp set to close or advance the goal but it had no effect.

lean_failure_modes.label: Tactic failed
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: A specific tactic raised an error (e.g. split_ifs with no if-then-else, a rewrite whose motive is not type-correct, a `show` whose pattern is not definitionally equal). The captured detail is the offending tactic.

lean_failure_modes.label: Unsolved goals
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: The proof script parsed and elaborated but left goals open. The model's reasoning was on track but incomplete — the closest 'soft' failure to a passing proof.

lean_failure_modes.label: Other Lean error
lean_failure_modes.interpretation: A Lean error that does not match any known signature. Inspect manually and, if it recurs, add a signature above.
